title: Palantir's Role as Core Defense Infrastructure
text: Palantir has secured multibillion-dollar defense contracts positioning it as a critical intelligence infrastructure provider. The company holds a $10 billion contract with the Army for its future software and data needs, along with significant agreements with the Department of Defense, Immigration & Customs Enforcement, Department of Homeland Security, Internal Revenue Service, and Department of Transportation. Founded by Peter Thiel and former PayPal executives, Palantir developed its Gotham Platform with funding from In-Q-Tel, the CIA's venture capital arm. The platform aggregates siloed data sources to map relationships between people, places, things, and events, enabling pattern recognition and actionable intelligence. The CIA began using Gotham in 2008. Beyond government work, Palantir has successfully extended into the commercial sector through its Foundry platform and Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP). The company has demonstrated 70% revenue growth in Q4, with U.S. commercial revenue surging 137%. Its customer count climbed 34% while its net dollar retention was a robust 139%. While trading at a forward price-to-sales multiple of 46, the analyst suggests Palantir could eventually become one of the world's largest companies due to its strategic positioning in both government and commercial AI applications.
